report_name: Researchers Detail New 'Gines' Ransomware Variant Linked to Makop Family

threat_actors.name: Gines operators
threat_actors.description: The operators of the Gines ransomware, a variant of the Makop family. They employ a double-extortion model, exfiltrating data before encrypting files on Windows systems.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Gines ransomware encrypts files on local Windows systems and mapped network shares, appending the '.gines' extension to them.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: As part of its double-extortion model, the Gines ransomware exfiltrates sensitive data from the victim's network before initiating the encryption process.

attack_patterns.name: Defacement
attack_patterns.description: The Gines ransomware drops a ransom note named '+README-WARNING+.txt' in directories and may modify the desktop wallpaper to display a warning.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Gines ransomware is associated with techniques to inhibit system recovery, such as using 'wmic shadowcopy delete' to remove Volume Shadow Copies.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The Gines ransomware operators use an Outlook email address for command and control and for victims to initiate contact, leveraging mail protocols.

malware.name: Gines
malware.description: A new ransomware strain and variant of the Makop family that targets Windows systems. It performs double extortion by exfiltrating data before encrypting files, appending the '.gines' extension, and dropping a ransom note named '+README-WARNING+.txt'.

malware.name: Makop
malware.description: A ransomware family of which the Gines ransomware is a new variant. Makop variants often use RDP compromise or phishing for initial access.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The Windows Management Instrumentation Command-line utility is likely used by Gines ransomware to delete Volume Shadow Copies to inhibit system recovery.

infrastructure.name: Gines Ransomware Contact Service
infrastructure.description: An Outlook email address (ginesomna@outlook.com) is used by the Gines ransomware operators for victims to initiate contact and negotiate ransom payment.

indicators.name: Gines Ransomware Ransom Note Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Gines Ransomware Ransom Note Creation
id: 2b0c1d3e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the Gines ransomware ransom note file '+README-WARNING+.txt'.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/research/weekly-intelligence-report-29-may-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\+README-WARNING+.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the '+README-WARNING+.txt' ransom note file associated with Gines ransomware.

indicators.name: Gines Ransomware Contact Email
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Gines Ransomware Contact Email In Network Traffic
id: 8a7b6c5d-4e3f-4a2b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the Gines ransomware contact email address 'ginesomna@outlook.com' in network traffic, potentially indicating communication with the attackers.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/research/weekly-intelligence-report-29-may-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1071.003
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'ginesomna@outlook.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the Gines ransomware operator's contact email address in network traffic.

indicators.name: Gines/Makop Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
id: f1e2d3c4-b5a6-4987-8fed-cba987654321
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'wmic' to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware like Gines/Makop to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.cyfirma.com/research/weekly-intelligence-report-29-may-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line execution of 'wmic shadowcopy delete', a technique used by Gines ransomware to impede recovery.

relationships.source_name: Gines/Makop Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
Gines/Makop Ransomware Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
